normalization.notes: Use ASCII dot, hyphen, spaces, and slash as the review notation until a richer timing model exists. Do not silently fold dash variants or treat textual notation as the canonical signal.

segmentation.pedagogical_units: dit, dah, intra-character gap, character gap, word gap, letters, numerals, punctuation, and prosigns
segmentation.notes: Morse is a time-based signaling notation. Text dot-dash strings are authoring aids and must eventually map to timed audio, light, or haptic patterns.

sources.notes: International Morse reference for code assignments and spacing concepts. Do not copy standard tables into shipped lessons without review.

sources.license: Unicode License reviewed for metadata use
sources.notes: Symbol metadata for display choices such as dot, hyphen, slash, and timing labels.
Radio-learning context and practice conventions. Keep discovery-only until terms are approved.
Short dot-dash and future timed exercises authored by Glyphin and reviewed by operators.
